source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Order positive and negative integers, use the number line as a model for ordering real numbers, and apply the four operations to integers.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This Year 7 bridge pack extends whole-number ordering through zero into signed calculation, with subtraction represented as adding the opposite rather than memorising isolated sign rules.

objective.child_goal: I can order and calculate with negative numbers, and explain each move on a number line.
objective.statement: Compare, order and calculate with positive and negative integers, using number-line and contextual models to justify signed addition and subtraction.
misconceptions: Thinks a number with a larger magnitude is always the greater number, so says -8 is greater than -3.
Treats the negative sign as an instruction to subtract rather than as part of a signed number.
Assumes subtraction always means move left, including when subtracting a negative number.
Uses the rule 'two negatives make a positive' without identifying the operation or explaining why.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A whole number that may be positive, negative or zero.
vocabulary.audio_script: Integers are whole numbers on both sides of zero, including zero.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Marked whole-number points extending in both directions from zero.

vocabulary.audio_script: A negative number lies below zero or to the left of zero on a number line.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A signed value positioned left of zero and below sea level.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A number the same distance from zero on the other side, such as 6 and -6.
vocabulary.audio_script: Opposite numbers are equally far from zero in different directions.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A mirror pair of points around zero.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A number's distance from zero, without direction.
vocabulary.audio_script: Absolute value tells us distance from zero, so it is never negative.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Equal-length distance arrows from zero to -5 and 5.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The distance or change between two values.
vocabulary.audio_script: Difference can be shown as the distance between two points on a number line.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A bracket spanning two signed positions.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Place temperatures, floors and elevations on one vertical scale.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Reactivate negative numbers as values below a reference point rather than as a special set of labels.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Zero is a reference point. Values can extend in both directions from it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Temperature, lift-floor and elevation cards align to a shared signed scale.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Compare signed numbers by locating them, not by looking only at their digits.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish that position determines order and that values increase to the right.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The number farther to the right is greater, even when both numbers are negative.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A horizontal number line scans from -10 through zero to 10.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Why is -3 greater than -8 even though 8 is the larger digit?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model comparison using position, distance and context while separating magnitude from order.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Negative three is closer to zero and lies to the right of negative eight, so negative three is greater.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Points at -8 and -3 appear with distance bars to zero and a greater-than arrow.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rewrite each subtraction as adding the opposite, then show the movement.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect subtraction of signed integers to a consistent number-line model.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Subtracting a number is equivalent to adding its opposite. Subtracting negative six means adding positive six.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The expression -4 - (-6) transforms into -4 + 6 before the arrow moves right.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect rote left-for-subtraction reasoning and check whether the learner reads the sign of the number being subtracted.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: First change subtract negative five into add positive five, then move five places right.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three candidate arrows start at 2 and show different directions and lengths.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Lyra why 'two negatives make a positive' is not a complete explanation for every calculation.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Replace an overgeneralised slogan with operation-specific reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Which negative is the operation, which negative belongs to the number, and what equivalent addition does the expression show?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion labels the subtraction sign and the sign of the second integer separately.

manipulatives.purpose: Coordinate order, distance, contextual change and signed operations on horizontal and vertical number lines.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners place values, compare positions, mirror opposites, transform subtraction into adding the opposite, drag directional arrows and inspect start-change-end records.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large targets, keyboard stepping, spoken coordinates, high-contrast direction patterns, optional integer chips and static step tables provide SEND alternatives to drag and animated movement.

manipulatives.purpose: Apply signed change through meaningful temperature, elevation, score and financial contexts without reducing the task to decorative arithmetic.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose a start state, sequence signed changes, predict the finish, inspect a mission log and diagnose whether an error came from direction, magnitude or operation.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Uses spoken start-change-end records, keyboard step controls, high-contrast arrows, optional integer chips and a motion-free transaction table.

practice.interleaving_notes: Secure comparison and movement through zero before mixing all addition and subtraction structures; interleave temperature, elevation and abstract items so context supports rather than replaces signed-number reasoning.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Place, compare and order integers across zero and within the negative region.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: integer set + scaled or partially labelled line + place/order/inequality response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Vary scale intervals and include close negative pairs; do not let visual spacing reveal answers accidentally.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Represent signed changes in temperature, elevation, score and account contexts without changing the underlying mathematics.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start value + signed change + horizontal or vertical model + end-value and explanation prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use neutral, age-appropriate contexts and label the reference point clearly.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Calculate signed addition and subtraction by transforming subtraction into adding the opposite.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: integer expression + equivalent-addition choice + directional arrow + integer result
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Balance all sign structures and include movement that crosses zero in both directions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Select and explain valid reasoning for signed operations instead of relying on unqualified sign slogans.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: worked or incorrect expression + four explanations + operation/sign-role feedback
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Every distractor must diagnose a specific sign, direction, magnitude or operation misconception.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain fluent comparison, opposites, differences and short signed calculations through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: scheduler-selected one-step prompt + optional mini line + exact integer response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Mix representation and operation types while keeping retrieval items brief and untimed by default.

body.prompt: Place -7, -2, 0 and 4 on the number line, then order them from least to greatest.

hints: Values increase as you move right.
Among negative numbers, the value farther left is smaller.
question_variants.explanation: -7 is farthest left, followed by -2, then 0 and 4, so -7 < -2 < 0 < 4.

body.prompt: At dawn the temperature is -3°C. By midday it is 5°C. Show the change and find how much the temperature increased.

hints: Count 3 degrees from -3 to 0, then 5 more to 5.
The difference is the total distance between the two values.
question_variants.explanation: The temperature rises 3°C to reach zero and another 5°C to reach 5°C, for an increase of 8°C.

body.prompt: Calculate -4 - (-6). First choose the equivalent addition, then show the movement on the number line.

hints: Subtracting negative six is adding its opposite, positive six.
Start at -4 and move six places right.
question_variants.explanation: -4 - (-6) is equivalent to -4 + 6. Moving six places right from -4 lands on 2.

body.prompt: Which explanation correctly compares -8 and -3?
choices: -8 is greater because 8 is greater than 3.
-3 is greater because it lies to the right of -8 on the number line.
They are equal because both are negative.
-3 is greater because negative signs can be ignored when comparing.

expected_answer.value: -3 is greater because it lies to the right of -8 on the number line.

hints: Compare the positions of the complete signed numbers.
The greater number is farther right.
question_variants.explanation: -3 is greater than -8 because -3 lies farther right and is closer to zero; the digit size alone does not determine the order of negative numbers.

body.prompt: Expedition number-line route 1: place and order -12, -5, 0, 3, 10 from least to greatest.
body.representation: horizontal line with labelled zero, equal intervals and persistent arrows
body.concrete_scaffold: tactile floor line with a raised zero marker, equal interval notches and movable signed cards
body.visual_scaffold: equal-interval number line or vertical scale with persistent zero, signed arrows and labels; colour is never the only cue
body.tactile_scaffold: raised zero marker, interval notches, embossed plus/minus cards and signed counters
body.text_scaffold: START, OPERATION or CHANGE, END and DISTANCE table with one signed step per line
body.static_alternative: numbered positions, arrow description and calculation table containing the complete task without motion
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant before-and-after states with persistent arrow labels and no automatic travel animation
sentence_stems: The sign belongs to ___ and means ___.
I start at ___ and move ___, so I finish at ___.
The distance is ___ but the directed change is ___.
body.context_policy: fictional, neutral expedition, temperature, elevation or score setting; no real debt, loss, competition or public ranking is required

hints: Equal intervals represent equal changes; values increase to the right or upward.
For negative values, compare complete positions rather than the sizes of the digits.
question_variants.explanation: -12 < -5 < 0 < 3 < 10 because this is their left-to-right order on a line with equal intervals. Distance from zero is not the same as numerical order.

feedback.correct: Expedition coordinate verified. -12 < -5 < 0 < 3 < 10 because this is their left-to-right order on a line with equal intervals. Distance from zero is not the same as numerical order.
feedback.try_again: No timer and no lost progress. Equal intervals represent equal changes; values increase to the right or upward.
feedback.misconception: The 'magnitude confused with order' route conflicts with the signed model. For negative values, compare complete positions rather than the sizes of the digits.
feedback.strategy: Anchor zero and the scale step first, place one value at a time, then read from the least position to the greatest.
feedback.operation_check: Name the operation, keep signs attached to numbers, and use adding the opposite only when rewriting subtraction.
feedback.distance_change_check: Distance is non-negative separation; directed change is end minus start and keeps direction.

gamification.objective: Restore one expedition coordinate by justifying order, direction, endpoint or distance.
gamification.strategy_choice: Choose line, lift map, signed counters or static cards; route choice and replay are not scored.

body.concrete_scaffold: tactile floor line with a raised zero marker, equal interval notches and movable signed cards
body.visual_scaffold: equal-interval number line or vertical scale with persistent zero, signed arrows and labels; colour is never the only cue
body.tactile_scaffold: raised zero marker, interval notches, embossed plus/minus cards and signed counters
body.text_scaffold: START, OPERATION or CHANGE, END and DISTANCE table with one signed step per line
body.static_alternative: numbered positions, arrow description and calculation table containing the complete task without motion
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant before-and-after states with persistent arrow labels and no automatic travel animation
sentence_stems: The sign belongs to ___ and means ___.
I start at ___ and move ___, so I finish at ___.
The distance is ___ but the directed change is ___.
body.context_policy: fictional, neutral expedition, temperature, elevation or score setting; no real debt, loss, competition or public ranking is required

hints: Equal intervals represent equal changes; values increase to the right or upward.
For negative values, compare complete positions rather than the sizes of the digits.
question_variants.explanation: -19 < -13 < -8 < -3 because this is their left-to-right order on a line with equal intervals. Distance from zero is not the same as numerical order.

feedback.correct: Expedition coordinate verified. -19 < -13 < -8 < -3 because this is their left-to-right order on a line with equal intervals. Distance from zero is not the same as numerical order.
feedback.try_again: No timer and no lost progress. Equal intervals represent equal changes; values increase to the right or upward.
feedback.misconception: The 'magnitude confused with order' route conflicts with the signed model. For negative values, compare complete positions rather than the sizes of the digits.
feedback.strategy: Anchor zero and the scale step first, place one value at a time, then read from the least position to the greatest.
feedback.operation_check: Name the operation, keep signs attached to numbers, and use adding the opposite only when rewriting subtraction.
feedback.distance_change_check: Distance is non-negative separation; directed change is end minus start and keeps direction.

gamification.objective: Restore one expedition coordinate by justifying order, direction, endpoint or distance.
gamification.strategy_choice: Choose line, lift map, signed counters or static cards; route choice and replay are not scored.

body.concrete_scaffold: signed counters with zero-pair frames beside a number line showing the equivalent-addition movement
body.visual_scaffold: equal-interval number line or vertical scale with persistent zero, signed arrows and labels; colour is never the only cue
body.tactile_scaffold: raised zero marker, interval notches, embossed plus/minus cards and signed counters
body.text_scaffold: START, OPERATION or CHANGE, END and DISTANCE table with one signed step per line
body.static_alternative: numbered positions, arrow description and calculation table containing the complete task without motion
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant before-and-after states with persistent arrow labels and no automatic travel animation
sentence_stems: The sign belongs to ___ and means ___.
I start at ___ and move ___, so I finish at ___.
The distance is ___ but the directed change is ___.
body.context_policy: fictional, neutral expedition, temperature, elevation or score setting; no real debt, loss, competition or public ranking is required

hints: If the operation is subtraction, keep the first number and add the opposite of the second number.
The sign of the added value determines movement: positive right, negative left.
question_variants.explanation: 3 + (-6) becomes 3 + (-6). Starting at 3, the signed movement lands on -3; the operation and the signs keep their distinct roles.

feedback.correct: Expedition coordinate verified. 3 + (-6) becomes 3 + (-6). Starting at 3, the signed movement lands on -3; the operation and the signs keep their distinct roles.
feedback.try_again: No timer and no lost progress. If the operation is subtraction, keep the first number and add the opposite of the second number.
feedback.misconception: The 'addition always increases' route conflicts with the signed model. The sign of the added value determines movement: positive right, negative left.
feedback.strategy: Separate operation and number signs, rewrite as addition, then verify the same result with counters and one line movement.
feedback.operation_check: Name the operation, keep signs attached to numbers, and use adding the opposite only when rewriting subtraction.
feedback.distance_change_check: Distance is non-negative separation; directed change is end minus start and keeps direction.

question_variants.explanation: The opposite of -8 is +8, so -5 - (-8) = -5 + 8 = 3. The direction follows the equivalent addition, not a slogan about adjacent signs.

feedback.correct: Expedition coordinate verified. The opposite of -8 is +8, so -5 - (-8) = -5 + 8 = 3. The direction follows the equivalent addition, not a slogan about adjacent signs.
feedback.try_again: No timer and no lost progress. Name the operation before applying any sign rule or movement direction.
feedback.misconception: The 'subtraction always left' route conflicts with the signed model. Check the complete signed values on a line and use a counterexample if the claim uses always or never.
feedback.strategy: Underline the operation sign and circle signs belonging to numbers, then rewrite subtraction as adding the opposite before reasoning.
feedback.operation_check: Name the operation, keep signs attached to numbers, and use adding the opposite only when rewriting subtraction.
feedback.distance_change_check: Distance is non-negative separation; directed change is end minus start and keeps direction.

hints: Use the mini line, signed counters or opposite card before entering the integer.
Keep direction, endpoint and distance as separate ideas, and check the sign of the final value.
question_variants.explanation: Adding two negative values combines their negative changes: four left and six more left gives -10.

feedback.correct: Expedition coordinate verified. Adding two negative values combines their negative changes: four left and six more left gives -10.
feedback.try_again: No timer and no lost progress. Use the mini line, signed counters or opposite card before entering the integer.
feedback.misconception: The 'two negatives always positive' route conflicts with the signed model. Keep direction, endpoint and distance as separate ideas, and check the sign of the final value.
feedback.strategy: Choose one strategy route, show one movement or zero-pair step, then enter the signed integer without a timer.
feedback.operation_check: Name the operation, keep signs attached to numbers, and use adding the opposite only when rewriting subtraction.
feedback.distance_change_check: Distance is non-negative separation; directed change is end minus start and keeps direction.

gamification.objective: Restore one expedition coordinate by justifying order, direction, endpoint or distance.
gamification.strategy_choice: Choose line, lift map, signed counters or static cards; route choice and replay are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a number with a larger magnitude is always the greater number, so says -8 is greater than -3.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner compares the unsigned digits and reverses the order of two negative integers.
repair_sequence: Place both complete signed numbers on a number line.
State that values increase to the right.
Compare order with absolute-value distance as a separate property.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner orders a new set containing at least three negative integers and explains one comparison by position.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats the negative sign as an instruction to subtract rather than as part of a signed number.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner reads -5 as an incomplete operation or drops the sign when placing or calculating.
repair_sequence: Contrast the sign of a number with the operation between two numbers.
Use start-change-end language to identify each role in a contextual calculation.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner preserves signs while placing values and distinguishes the operation sign from number signs.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes subtraction always means move left, including when subtracting a negative number.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner moves left from the first value in every subtraction expression.
repair_sequence: Rewrite subtraction as adding the opposite.
Identify the direction of the equivalent signed addition.
Check the result using the inverse operation or a contextual change.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner correctly models and solves a subtraction of a negative integer in a fresh example.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses the rule 'two negatives make a positive' without identifying the operation or explaining why.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner applies the slogan to addition, comparison or unrelated adjacent negative signs.
repair_sequence: Label each symbol as an operation or a number sign.
Transform only subtract-negative expressions into adding the opposite.
Explain the resulting direction on the number line.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner gives an operation-specific explanation instead of a free-floating sign rule.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm grid, steady arrows and no rapid scrolling, countdowns or alarm-style feedback.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Offer static start-change-end frames and numbered hops instead of animated travel along the line.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read signed numbers with deliberate pauses, for example 'subtract negative six', and narrate start, direction and distance.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Keep contexts short, show the expression beside the model and use consistent icons for start, change and end.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Reveal one decision at a time: locate, choose equivalent operation, choose direction, calculate and check.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow integer chips, a vertical scale or a written step table as alternatives to mental movement.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Preserve correct positioning or direction as partial evidence and route only the failed sign decision to repair.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach negative, positive, below, above, increase, decrease, opposite, difference and greater than with paired visual examples.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate ordering and addition or subtraction of integers with a number-line justification; secure requires flexible reasoning across abstract and contextual representations, including subtracting negative integers and diagnosing sign-rule errors.
evidence.parent_summary: Can compare negative numbers and calculate signed changes, including movement through zero and subtracting a negative number.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Orders integers by position, distinguishes signs from operations, rewrites subtraction as adding the opposite and explains direction and distance.
next_actions: Interleave signed arithmetic with algebraic term collection and coordinate work.
Introduce multiplication and division of signed integers after addition and subtraction explanations are secure.
Route repeated magnitude errors back to ordering before increasing operation complexity.

qa.notes: Year 7 negative numbers reaches the 240-item pilot target with four preserved curated variants and 236 deterministic review candidates covering ordering, number lines, integer addition and subtraction, neutral contexts, directed change, distance, operation reasoning and misconceptions. Every generated item includes labelled visual, tactile/concrete, static text and spoken routes; keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, oral and partner-mediated interactions; rich strategy and misconception feedback; and private expedition progress without timers, speed scores, lives, streak pressure, leaderboards or peer comparison. Independent mathematics, teacher, SEND, accessibility, safeguarding, renderer and pilot review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
